pr.title: Initial work on func pointer tables
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Converted many raw function-address entries in assembly data tables into symbolic function references and added missing global function labels in asm/text_2.s so those pointers could resolve by symbol. The PR is an early data-naming/function-pointer-table cleanup: it makes pointer tables more readable and safer for later decomp work without materially changing executable logic.

key_files.role: Replaced raw .4byte function addresses with func_* symbolic references across data tables, including tables at lbl_803C06E8, lbl_803C07AC, lbl_803C11D8, and the large lbl_803C2800 entry table.
Added .global declarations and func_* labels at existing instruction addresses so data-table references could use function symbols instead of numeric addresses.

what_changed: In asm/data.s, many entries like `.4byte 0x80071028` were changed to `.4byte func_80071028`.
Two adjacent pointer tables at lbl_803C06E8 and lbl_803C07AC were converted from raw addresses to func_* references for functions in the 0x80071028-0x8007320C range.
Additional scattered data-table entries were symbolized, including references such as func_800E08CC, func_8013C340, func_8011493C, func_8014A828, func_80142640, and func_8014F98C.

The large table at lbl_803C2800, whose comments identify per-entry function slots as Anim, Input, Action, Collision, and Camera, received many symbolic function pointer replacements such as func_800D3E00, func_800C95F4, func_800CCA00, and repeated func_800761C8 camera-slot references.

In asm/text_2.s, existing code boundaries were marked with `.global func_*` and `func_*:` labels, for example func_8007121C, func_8007162C, func_8007168C, func_80073008, func_80073118, func_8007320C, and func_80096AA0.

The diff records 2 changed files, 820 added lines, and 505 deleted lines; most data changes are one-for-one raw-address-to-symbol substitutions, while text_2.s mostly adds labels to pre-existing assembly.
smart_moves: Used symbolic function names in `.4byte` data entries rather than leaving raw absolute addresses, making function pointer tables searchable and less error-prone.
Paired data-table symbolization with adding the required `.global` labels in text_2.s, avoiding unresolved references while preserving the original assembly bodies.
Kept the pointer-table layout intact: NULL entries, table labels, entry ordering, and existing comments such as the Anim/Input/Action/Collision/Camera slots were preserved.
Started with address-derived func_* names where semantic names were not yet known, which is appropriate for early decomp cleanup because it improves linkage without inventing meaning.
Converted repeated function pointers consistently, especially the recurring func_800761C8 entries in the large action-style table.
decomp_lessons: When a data table is known or suspected to contain function pointers, prefer `.4byte func_ADDRESS` over `.4byte 0xADDRESS`; this exposes references to tooling and future renaming passes.
Before symbolizing function-pointer data, ensure the target assembly locations have matching labels and `.global` declarations in the text file.
Address-derived `func_XXXXXXXX` labels are useful intermediate names: they are stable, searchable, and can later be renamed semantically once the function is understood.
Function pointer tables can be improved independently of full C decompilation; even assembly-only symbolization helps cross-reference analysis.
Preserving table shape and comments while changing only pointer spelling is a low-risk way to improve data readability without affecting matching.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions are named with the existing decomp convention `func_XXXXXXXX`, using the uppercase hexadecimal runtime address, e.g. `func_8007121C` and `func_8013C340`.
Unknown data labels remain in `lbl_XXXXXXXX` form, e.g. `lbl_803C06E8`, `lbl_803C07AC`, and `lbl_803C2800`.
Null function slots are represented as `NULL`, while actual function pointers use symbolic `func_*` labels.
The PR does not introduce semantic function names; it relies on address-based placeholders only.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace raw `.4byte 0x80......` entries with `.4byte func_......` when the value points at code.
Add `.global func_XXXXXXXX` plus a matching `func_XXXXXXXX:` label at the existing instruction address in asm/text_2.s.
Do not move or rewrite instruction bodies when adding labels; the text_2.s changes are label-boundary annotations over existing assembly.
Leave unresolved or not-yet-symbolized addresses as raw `.4byte 0x...` where the diff did not establish a function label.
Use repeated table patterns and comments, such as `# Functions (Anim, Input, Action, Collision, Camera)`, to identify which `.4byte` fields are function pointers.

review_feedback.detail: The provided PR slice has no issue comments, review comments, or reviews, so no reviewer rationale or requested changes can be extracted.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_803C2800 or convert the Anim/Input/Action/Collision/Camera entries into a C struct.
Search for subsequent semantic renames of func_800761C8 and other repeatedly used table callbacks.
Check whether raw `.4byte 0x80......` entries left in the same tables were later proven to be functions or non-function data.
Look for later PRs that move these text_2.s assembly labels into C source or header declarations.
